candidate_body_preview: Simplify the expression \(2025n - 15n\) by factoring out \(n\): \(2025n - 15n = (2025 - 15)n = 2010n\). Thus, the condition becomes: if \(n \in S\), then every positive divisor of \(2010n\) is in \(S\).
Factor 2010 into primes: \(2010 = 2 \times 1005 = 2 \times 3 \times 335 = 2 \times 3 \times 5 \times 67\). Thus, the prime factors of 2010 are exactly 2, 3, 5, and 67.

For any positive integer \(n\), the prime factors of \(2010n\) are contained in the union of the prime factors of \(2010\) and the prime factors of \(n\). Since the prime factors of \(2010\) are exactly \(2, 3, 5, 67\),
